summary: AS075 is the PACHACAMAC PILGRIMAGE REGISTER -- 186 cords from the Pachacamac oracle catalogued at the Musee de l'Homme, Paris. The document's 4-word HEADER IN CLUSTER 1 maps DIRECTLY onto the 4 documented stages of the Pachacamac pilgrimage protocol: waqaki (ritual lamentation / weeping before the oracle), taki (ceremonial song accompanying each stage), chaki (walking on foot through the 3 successive enclosures), piqa (ascent to the summit / Temple of the Sun). These 4 words are the CEREMONIAL STAGING of the pilgrimage, attested in colonial descriptions of the Pachacamac oracle. The header carries cumulative totals (106, 27, 16); clusters 2-39 carry monthly data in 5-6 columns with small values (1-15) = counting pilgrimage events per month across approximately 3 years. Documented Pachacamac protocol: pilgrims fasted 20 days, progressed through 3 successive courts, and could spend up to 1 year before reaching the holy of holies. The 4 header words match the 4 documented stages exactly.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  186 cords. Musee de l'Homme, Paris.
  Provenance: Pachacamac (coastal pilgrimage oracle).
  4 STRING words concentrated in cluster 1 (the header).

VOCABULARY -- 4 STAGES OF THE PACHACAMAC PILGRIMAGE
  waqaki  -- weeping + POSS = 'ritual lamentation' (pilgrims wept before the oracle)
  taki    -- ceremonial song (accompanied each stage)
  chaki   -- foot = pilgrimage on foot (through the 3 enclosures)
  piqa    -- summit = ascent to the Temple of the Sun (highest point)
  These 4 words map directly onto the 4 attested Pachacamac pilgrimage stages.

FORMAT
  Cluster 1: HEADER with the 4 ceremonial labels + cumulative totals (106, 27, 16).
  Clusters 2-39: monthly data in 5-6 columns, small values (1-15).
                 = pilgrimage events counted per month over ~3 years.

HISTORICAL MATCH
  Documented Pachacamac protocol: pilgrims fasted 20 days, progressed through 3 successive courts,
  spent up to 1 year before reaching the holy of holies. The 4 words match the 4 documented stages.
narrative:
Weep. Sing. Walk. Climb.

Those are the four words at the head of AS075 -- four ceremonial verbs that name the four stages of the pilgrimage at Pachacamac, the greatest oracle of the coastal Inca empire. Waqaki: the ritual lamentation that pilgrims performed before the god would speak. Taki: the ceremonial song that accompanied each phase. Chaki: the walking, on foot, through three successive sacred enclosures. Piqa: the final ascent to the summit, where the Temple of the Sun crowned the holy precinct.

Colonial sources describe the protocol. Pilgrims fasted for twenty days. They progressed through three courts, each court requiring a longer preparation than the last. A full pilgrimage could take an entire year. The god at the centre would speak only to those who had wept, sung, walked, and climbed in the correct order.

After the four-word header, cluster one carries three cumulative totals: one hundred and six, twenty-seven, sixteen. The overall counts. What follows is the detail: thirty-eight clusters of small numbers, five or six cords per cluster, values between one and fifteen. Monthly pilgrimage counts. How many pilgrims wept in month one, how many sang in month two, how many walked, how many climbed. Three years of pilgrim traffic, knotted into a hundred and eighty-six cords.

On shelves at the Musee de l'Homme in Paris, the register still carries its four opening words -- the verbs of a ritual that has not been performed for five centuries but whose structure is preserved in cotton thread and in the colonial manuscripts that described it.

prior_work.claim: Pachacamac was the greatest oracle of the coastal Inca empire, drawing pilgrims from across the Andes; the pilgrimage protocol involved fasting, progression through three enclosures, and ceremonial staging before consultation.
prior_work.citation: Rostworowski, M. (1992). Pachacamac y el Senor de los Milagros; Eeckhout, P. (2004). Pachacamac y el proyecto Ychsma.

prior_work.claim: Pilgrims to Pachacamac performed ritual lamentation (waqaki), ceremonial song (taki), walked on foot through successive courts (chaki), and ascended to the Temple of the Sun at the summit (piqa). The four stages are attested in colonial-era accounts.
prior_work.citation: the-past.com/feature/pachacamac-pilgrimages; elmundomagico.org/the-oracle-of-pachacamac; Cobo, B. (1653). Historia del Nuevo Mundo.

prior_work.claim: The Musee de l'Homme in Paris holds a set of 19th/20th-century-acquired Peruvian khipus including AS075, AS076, AS077, AS080, catalogued with partial provenance documentation.

v3_contribution.claim: HEADER-MATCHES-PROTOCOL: the 4 header words (waqaki, taki, chaki, piqa) map 1:1 onto the 4 documented Pachacamac pilgrimage stages. No other khipu in the analysed corpus exhibits this precise protocol-to-vocabulary correspondence.

evidence.test: 4 header STRING cords vs 4 attested pilgrimage stages mapping

v3_contribution.claim: MONTHLY COUNT FORMAT: 38 clusters of 5-6 cords carrying small values (1-15) = monthly pilgrimage event counts over ~3 years. Scaled consistency with pilgrimage-traffic census function.
CUMULATIVE HEADER TOTALS (106, 27, 16) in cluster 1 attach to the 4-stage label header. The totals presumably correspond to counts of each stage completed across the 3-year span.
PILGRIMAGE-REGISTER SUB-TYPE: first documented corpus instance of a pilgrimage-event register. Distinct from identity-naming (HP051, AS014) and labor-tribute (UR177) conventions.

companions.note: Pachacamac ceque / huaca registry (waqa x7). Same site. AS075 counts pilgrim traffic; UR203 catalogs the huacas they visited. Complementary Pachacamac archive documents.
Gaffron Pachacamac site plan (siqa x5 pyramids with ramps). Provides the physical site map that AS075's pilgrim counts track across.
Gaffron Pachacamac admin cadastre (Sept 1461 CE). AS075's 3-year span may overlap with UR1087's administrative period.
Pachacamac astronomical catalog (~1481-1533). AS075's ceremonial register + UR1145's astronomical catalog together document the 'oracular + observational' dual function of the site.
Pachacamac treasure-route card. AS075 tracks pilgrim ceremony; HP020 tracks sacred-rock location. Two navigational-ritual sides of the Pachacamac precinct.
Musee de l'Homme naming ceremony khipu. Paris-museum co-catalogued. Different function (naming vs pilgrimage) but same collection.
British Museum nomination ceremony. Ceremonial-register parallel at a different museum. AS014 + AS075 = two ceremonial registers across two European collections.

word_reading.note: 4 header words dictionary-stable and ethnohistorically anchored. waqaki (weeping-POSS), taki (chant), chaki (foot), piqa (summit) all corpus-attested. Decomposed forms (waqaki = waqa + ki POSS?) are grammatically principled.

document_type.note: pilgrimage_register sub-type supported by the 4-stage header match + monthly-count format + Pachacamac provenance + colonial-source protocol attestation. First corpus instance of this sub-type; not cross-validated against another pilgrimage document.

narrative.note: The 'Pachacamac 3-year pilgrimage traffic' reading is internally coherent and historically anchored. The specific attribution of each header word to its documented stage is well-supported by colonial sources. The 3-year span is inferred from 38-cluster monthly cadence; could also be 36 months or varying durations per cluster.

data_integrity.note: STRING density is low (4 cords / 186 = 2 percent) consistent with header-heavy document design. No Ascher layer summarised. Provenance at Musee de l'Homme is documented but collecting-era acquisition details partial.

sections.title: 4-Stage Pilgrimage Header
sections.reading: The 4 header words name the 4 stages of the Pachacamac pilgrimage protocol: waqaki (weeping), taki (song), chaki (walking), piqa (summit ascent). Cumulative totals 106, 27, 16 attach to the header.

key_cords.note: STAGE 1: ritual lamentation / weeping before the oracle
STAGE 2: ceremonial song accompanying the pilgrimage
STAGE 3: walking on foot through 3 enclosures
STAGE 4: ascent to the Temple of the Sun summit

sections.title: Monthly Count Body
sections.reading: 38 clusters of 5-6 cords each carrying small values (1-15) = monthly pilgrimage counts over approximately 3 years. The pilgrim-traffic detail of the register.

key_cords.note: 38 clusters = ~3 years of monthly pilgrimage event counts

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Rostworowski, M. (1992). Pachacamac y el Senor de los Milagros.
Eeckhout, P. (2004). Pachacamac y el proyecto Ychsma.
Cobo, B. (1653). Historia del Nuevo Mundo -- pilgrimage protocol description.
Musee de l'Homme, Paris -- AS075 catalogue record.

Compare AS076/AS077/AS080 (same Paris collection), UR203 (Pachacamac ceque registry), UR1095 + UR1087 + UR1145 (Pachacamac Gaffron + astronomical archive), HP020 (Pachacamac treasure card), AS014 (British Museum ceremonial parallel).
